conventions: No item carries an ID or any other identifier; history lives in git and the session record.
Trivial fixes are done in the moment, never added here.
Delete-on-completion: a finished item is removed, not marked done. This file holds open items only.
System_Metadata bumps never reference backlog identifiers or items; bump text describes the change standalone.
The Critical priority tier is retired: critical issues are fixed in the moment, not parked here. Allowed priorities are High, Medium, Low.
Platform is for genuinely cross-cutting items only; anything owned by a single module or component uses its registry name.
component is validated against the components list; priority against High/Medium/Low; type against Build/Enhance/Bug/Design/Investigate.

label is a mandatory stable handle for each item: lowercase kebab-case, content-derived from the item's subject, unique within its component, and referenced as component/label (e.g. B2B/get-list-tickets); a label dies with its item and is never reused.
detail_link is a site-relative URL when the target is site-served, otherwise a plain-text repo reference that renders as text rather than as a link.

items.label: orphan-task-cleanup
items.summary: Stale FIRE_AND_FORGET tasks left in LAUNCHED/RUNNING after a crash or timeout are never auto-failed; add startup orphan cleanup and heartbeat timeout detection.
items.description: Two-part fix: (1) **Startup orphan cleanup** -- on engine start, auto-fail any tasks in LAUNCHED/RUNNING (guaranteed orphans from prior crash/restart). (2) **Heartbeat timeout detection** -- each cycle, check for LAUNCHED tasks exceeding ProcessRegistry timeout_seconds, auto-fail with alert. timeout_seconds must account for worst-case catch-up (XE Events observed at 22 min after 8-hour backlog). Need per-process timeout values in ProcessRegistry. Recurred 2026-03-10: Collect-XEEvents and Collect-ReplicationHealth both hung at 20:44, blocked 8 hours until manual cleanup.

items.label: execute-endpoint-rbac
items.summary: BDL import execute endpoints enforce only AD auth with no RBAC, letting any page-holder bypass the UI content filter by direct POST; add server-side department-scope validation.
items.description: `/api/bdl-import/execute` and related endpoints have no RBAC check -- only AD auth. A user with `/bdl-import` page access (any dept manager) can craft direct POSTs bypassing the UI content filter. Add server-side validation of the requester's `department_scope` against `Tools.AccessConfig` / `Tools.AccessFieldConfig` before executing imports. High-stakes gap from the 2026-05-15 RBAC audit.

items.label: thread-cap-stall-detection
items.summary: Investigate DM processing thread caps so stall detection for the PMT and NB collectors accounts for concurrency limits.
items.description: Investigate all DM processing thread caps via env_prfl_cnfg_ovrrd and config_item tables. Findings impact stall detection for both PMT and NB collectors.

items.label: webhook-alert-routing
items.summary: Move alert detection/escalation from MonitorConfig booleans to WebhookSubscription filters with alert_category populated (blocked on the Teams Workflow migration).
items.description: Move detection/escalation decision from MonitorConfig booleans to WebhookSubscription filters; populate alert_category on subscriptions. Dependent on Teams Workflow migration completing first.

items.label: ranked-cause-diagnosis
items.summary: Evolve sp_DiagnoseServerHealth to analyze time windows and output ranked probable causes with plain-English conclusions.
items.description: Analyze time windows and output ranked probable causes. Correlate HADR_SYNC waits + secondary PLE drops + scheduled job windows. Automated diagnosis with plain-English conclusions.

items.label: asset-registry-doc-pages
items.summary: Build the remaining Asset_Registry generated documentation pages (shared infrastructure, per-page component inventory, naming conventions, API endpoint reference).
items.description: Auto-generated pages from registry queries: CC Shared Infrastructure (shared components by file/section), Per-page Component Inventory, Naming Conventions (patterns derived from data), API Endpoint Reference. Pattern follows Platform_Registry's auto-generated tables. (Phases 1-4 of the original Asset_Registry buildout are complete; this generated-docs surface is what remains.)

items.label: scope-dimension-model
items.summary: RBAC cannot express per-scope page access, so department-scoped tool grants rely on nav-hiding plus content filtering with a URL-typing loophole; add a scope dimension to the model.
items.description: `RBAC_PermissionMapping` can't express "this scope of dept managers gets this page, others don't." For BDL, BI and Apps/Int managers should have access but BS and CR should not. Mitigated today by `show_in_nav=0` + content filtering via `Tools.AccessConfig`, but URL-typing remains a loophole. Proper fix: add `applies_to_scope` column on `RBAC_PermissionMapping` or similar. See snapshot doc section 9.2. **Elevated 2026-06-11:** the Profanity Redaction tool (B-089) is now a concrete consumer -- a live CRS5/DM write shared by Business Services and Applications & Integration, where "only BS and IT may use it" is enforced today by page-grants alone, not at the action level. The tier model (`operate`) can't express department identity, so the `redaction-apply` endpoints on both page routes are intentionally left unregistered (page-access is the only gate) pending this item. When B-020 lands, register per-department action gating for `redaction-apply` (both `/departmental/business-services` and `/departmental/applications-integration`). A real production-write use case now waits on this.

items.label: js-duplicate-identifier
items.summary: The JS populator lacks the cross-file duplicate top-level identifier detection the PS spec already has; add it (zone-scoped, all top-level identifiers) with an exemption for rule-mandated repeated names.
items.description: Bring the JS populator to parity with the PS pattern. DUPLICATE_FUNCTION_DEFINITION already exists in the PS spec (section 8.1: same function name declared by more than one PS file within the same zone) -- the JS populator has no equivalent. Add the code to the JS populator + JS spec, mirroring the PS rule's zone-scoping and name-match basis. Deliberate divergence from PS: the JS version flags any top-level identifier (functions, consts, classes, top-level state), not functions-only as PS does. Complements SHADOWS_SHARED_FUNCTION: SHADOWS catches a consumer redefining a name the shell exports; this catches two non-shell files in the same zone independently defining the same identifier. Exclude the shell file (shell->consumer is SHADOWS' job). Within-zone only -- cross-zone same-names are unrelated namespaces. Critical exemption -- rule-mandated repeated identifiers: in the docs zone every non-shell file shares the doc chrome prefix and page-file rules require each to declare its own doc_init (section 11.1), so doc_init legitimately appears in every docs non-shell file. The check must exempt identifiers that per-file rules mandate be present in every file of that kind (at minimum doc_init in the docs zone; generalize to any section 11.1/section 8-mandated fixed-name construct). Without this exemption the check fires on every conformant docs file. (This collision does not arise in cc, where per-page prefixes make each init distinct, e.g. bch_init vs srv_init.) No duplication is otherwise sanctioned -- the deprecated engine-events.js special-casing is being removed, not exempted. Not urgent -- duplicates carry no functional harm today. Surfaced 2026-06-21; exemption requirement surfaced during ddl-erd.js refactor.

items.label: execution-census
items.summary: Build a b2bi-native execution census as the primary B2B tracking surface (one row per instance from Sterling existence, enriched from Integration/DM) to close the silent child-fault gap; design phase.
items.description: Per Roadmap decision 2026-07-22 (B2B_Roadmap section 7.10): build a b2bi-native execution census as the primary B2B tracking surface -- one row per workflow instance sourced from b2bi existence (WF_INST_S + restore sibling), enriched with Integration/DM lifecycle content for the subset of runs that have it (Dirk's original unified-table formulation). Prompted by Step 09: invoked child-workflow faults fail silently -- no `Integration.ETL.tbl_B2B_CLIENTS_BATCH_STATUS` row, no `B2B.INT_PipelineTracking` row, green parent (anchor incident 8651070); ~4/day untracked. Completeness by construction (a row exists because the instance existed in Sterling, not because a workflow chose to write one). Design-phase first; investigation-first rules apply (Guidelines validation before any DDL, one object at a time). First design question: INT_PipelineTracking absorbed into the census vs. retained as an enrichment source -- the mirror stays necessary for DM reconciliation (its role changes, it is not deprecated). Surfaced 2026-07-22.

items.label: get-list-tickets
items.summary: FA_CLIENTS_GET_LIST's positional insert into the 11-column TICKETS table can never succeed, making GET_LIST faults ticket-invisible; fix with column-listed inserts in GET_LIST v20.
items.description: `FA_CLIENTS_GET_LIST`'s 9-value positional insert into `etl.tbl_B2B_CLIENTS_TICKETS` (11 columns since CLIENT_KEY was added ~Aug 2025) cannot succeed, so GET_LIST faults are ticket-invisible (Roadmap section 4.2a; Step 6G section 4 item 1). Fix: GET_LIST v20 with column-listed inserts (and MAIN's while there). Raised to High 2026-07-22: 2026-07-21 data shows the dispatcher itself faulting with the ticket path still broken. Sterling-side BPML change; owner Dirk/ops.

items.label: literal-token-burndown
items.summary: Site-wide burn-down of CSS hex/px literals to shared tokens; the color half is un-gated and in progress while size/radius stay gated behind the scale-design and matcher items.
items.description: Consolidates the file-side literal-tokenization work formerly split across B-011(a), B-012, B-054, and B-096. Gated behind the scale-design keystone (B-087) and the populator-matcher items (B-014 size sub-classification, B-097 dimension-family convention): scale decisions change what "the correct token" is, and the matcher changes what fires, so neither the bulk tokenize nor the residual exact-match resolution should run ahead of them. Method: tokenize hex/px literals against the **live** cc-shared.css token block, true purpose-match only (don't round to nearest tier); each one-off matching a token of a different role gets a per-case call (use token / promote a new purpose-named token / accept as deliberate local value). File-side, page-by-page; no populator change for the color side. **Color (was B-012):** ~105 rows, dominated by low-alpha rgba tints reused as badge/pill/card backgrounds and borders across nearly every page -- four recurring families `rgba(86,156,214,*)`, `rgba(78,201,176,*)`, `rgba(220,220,170,*)`, `rgba(244,135,113,*)` at alpha 0.08/0.15/0.3/0.4 (bulk tokenize-to-existing-tint). ~15 opaque one-off hexes are per-case: clear tokenize (`#4ec9b0` on idx-rebuild-progress-fill = accent-shared) vs likely-new-token-or-accept (`#22c55e`, `#6d3030`, `#ce9178`/`#f48771`, `#444`, `#2d2d2d`). **ServerHealth (was B-011a):** server-health.css hex/px literals, same method. **Platform (was B-054):** platform carries token-less page-local literals that read clean only because of the prior multi-line line-key gap; they resurface once the matcher work lands and fold into this pass. **Docs radius (was B-096):** border-radius across refactored docs files is partly tokenized (`--size-radius-default: 8px`) and partly raw (6/5/4/3/2px in docs-base.css, docs-erd.css). Two steps, one pass once more docs files are refactored: (1) design a deliberate radius scale in docs-base.css FOUNDATION (small named set chosen from the full cross-file inventory, not per-file); (2) snap standalone literals to the nearest scale value where it doesn't significantly alter the look (5px->4px imperceptible; larger jumps case-by-case), leaving genuinely-distinct values as deliberate literals or extending the scale. Per CSS spec radius rule (value used in 2+ places is a token; single-use may stay literal), confirm which recur vs. are single-use. ERD's 6/4/2 radii ship as conformant literals today (no matching token) and fold in here. Same approach likely applies to docs spacing literals and rgba tint-opacity -- evaluate in the same sweep. Today's residual literal drift (~137, cleanly-resolvable exact-matches left in place) is accepted as deliberate until B-087 lands and this burns it down. **Update 2026-06-23 -- color half un-gated and triggered.** The platform color-token change this session (`--color-status-online` added as the on/active green; `--color-status-critical` red change in progress) decoupled the **color** portion from the B-087/B-014 gate: color resolution depends on the color tokens being right, not the size scale, so the color sweep proceeds independently while size/radius stay gated. Changing token values surfaced the platform's de-facto colors as exact-match drift (`#ef4444` x39, `#4ade80` x31 are the two most-used colors site-wide, plus matches across admin/aai/bdl/jboss) -- ~140 color `DRIFT_HEX_LITERAL` rows now actionable. Census tooling: `Color_Literal_Census.sql` (5-section read-only roll-up, groups by value-as-written, zone='cc'). **Resolution model (this session):** a `DRIFT_HEX_LITERAL` row means a same-purpose token holds the same value -- repoint literal -> `var()`. Three sub-cases: (1) **clean exact-match, safe bulk-repoint** -- one same-purpose token, purpose fits selector (`#4ade80` on `.adm-*-success` -> `--color-status-online`; `#444` disabled-value -> `--color-status-disabled`); (2) **ambiguous double-match** -- value matches two same-purpose tokens (`#22c55e` matches BOTH `--color-status-idle` AND `--color-status-online`, online defined to idle's value this session), blocked until anchor-set (B-101) diverges them; (3) **purpose-question** -- matches a same-family token of arguably different role (`#ce9178` stage-badge vs `--color-text-pre`), repoint-if-same-purpose or add a new purpose-named token (B-101). **Key principle:** a literal that should stay a literal should NOT fire drift -- if it fires, repoint it (same purpose) or add a new chrome token for its distinct purpose and repoint to that; never spoil the value into a non-chrome cousin to silence the flag. A true false positive (value matching a genuinely UNRELATED-purpose token) is a matcher bug -> fix populator gating, not the file. Open/undecided drift is a valid holding state, not a failure. **Cousins** (`#22c55e`/`#16a34a`/`#4caf50`/`#dc2626`/`#f44747`/`#f14c4c`... a dozen+ reds/greens at non-identical values) do NOT exact-match and are NOT in this wave -- they need B-101 + B-102 first. Census: ~270 distinct color values site-wide vs target ~15-20; almost all LOCAL scope -- greenfield consolidation.

items.label: size-token-subfamilies
items.summary: Decide whether size literals need color-style purpose sub-families so value-coincidence collisions with unrelated size tokens stop firing false drift; populator change.
items.description: Size-side of the color-literal work just completed. Many size literals match a size token of unrelated purpose by value coincidence (`padding: 2px` vs `--size-radius-xs: 2px`; `font-size: 24px` vs `--font-size-h1: 24px`). Decide whether size warrants the two-tier treatment color got (spacing/radius/font-size/layout-dimension sub-families) or whether some are better resolved file-side by adopting the correct `var()`. Investigate-then-act; don't blanket-tokenize or blanket-suppress. Populator change. **Investigation 2026-06-09 (full-catalog drift scan, 355 drift rows of 2,771 literals):** size + font-size drift is ~250 rows -- the dominant share of all literal drift -- and is overwhelmingly collision-artifact, not real misses. Pattern: small-integer px on spacing/dimension props (`1px`/`2px`/`3px`/`4px` padding/margin/gap, `12`/`16`/`20`/`40`/`60px` padding/width/height/min-*) colliding with `--size-*` tokens of unrelated purpose; plus ~30 `font-size` rows (`10`/`11`/`13`/`14`/`24px`) colliding with `--font-size-*` of a different semantic role. Confirms the sub-classification path is the high-leverage fix: one populator change (split `size` into spacing/radius/dimension sub-families so a property matches only its own sub-family, mirroring the color-bg/border/text split already in `Get-PropertyTokenFamily`/`Get-TokenNameFamily`) clears ~250 rows at once. File-side resolution rejected for the bulk -- would touch ~250 declarations across ~20 files for value coincidences that aren't real token misses. Note: the matcher is property-FAMILY granular, not semantic-token granular, so same-family different-purpose tokens (e.g. `--color-bg-card` vs `--color-bg-card-hover`) still collide even within a sub-family; the sub-classification reduces but does not fully eliminate this.

items.label: matched-token-persistence
items.summary: Persist the matched token name on color/size literal-drift rows so the burn-down can self-classify safe-bulk-repoint vs needs-review without eyeballing each row.
items.description: Make `DRIFT_HEX_LITERAL`/`DRIFT_PX_LITERAL` self-classifying into safe-bulk-repoint vs needs-review, so the B-099 burn-down does not require eyeballing every row. **Verified (Populate-AssetRegistry-CSS.ps1, 2026-06-23):** the matcher (~lines 549-556) does value-AND-purpose matching at property-FAMILY granularity -- fires only when a shared token of the same family/purpose holds the same value (`Test-LiteralTokenFamilyMatch` gates family; role/state tokens match any color property; value via `ConvertTo-ColorKey`). It computes WHICH token matched (`$tokEntry`, line 555) but DISCARDS it -- line 559 writes only the bare code. Token name is available: the map builder (~lines 2105-2114) computes `$varName` to derive Family then drops it (entries store SourceFile/Value/Family, not Name). **Fix (two touch-points):** (1) add `Name = $varName` to the token map entry ~2110; (2) at ~557-560 persist `$tokEntry.Name` on the drifting row as a matched-token field. Transient drift-detail (set only on drifting rows, cleared on truncate-reload) -- NOT a permanent column (would be NULL across the table at steady state). **Enables WHERE-clause sorting:** safe-bulk-repoint = matched-token in anchor allowlist (B-101) AND single match; needs-review = token outside the set OR more than one same-purpose match (the double-match). **Scope pending:** first match only vs ALL same-purpose matches (latter makes double-match self-surfacing; recommended). Ceiling: isolates the residual needing judgment from the safe bulk; does NOT auto-decide selector-semantic cases -- below family granularity, stays human. Same root cause as B-014.

items.label: anchor-palette
items.summary: Consolidate the cc-shared.css color token set into a concise anchor palette (dedupe identical-value tokens, decide anchor hues, name un-tokenized purposes) as prerequisite to the cousin half of the burn-down.
items.description: Consolidate the `:root` color token set in cc-shared.css into a concise anchor palette BEFORE the cousin half of B-099 and before B-100's allowlist can be defined. Token set was harvested from existing file values -- incomplete in places, already duplicated. **Known duplication (this session):** `--color-status-idle` and `--color-status-online` both hold `#22c55e` (online added this session at idle's value so nothing repainted) -- identical-value same-purpose tokens make any `#22c55e` literal an ambiguous double-match, unresolvable until settled (decide idle-vs-online distinct, diverge e.g. online -> `#4ade80`). **Tasks:** (1) dedupe identical-value tokens (merge vs diverge per pair); (2) decide anchor red/green/amber/teal/grey and any severity vocabularies (does env test/stage/prod warrant own tokens vs reusing `--color-text-pre` -- the `#ce9178` stage-badge question); (3) name recurring literal purposes with no token yet (promote, don't spoil-to-cousin). Output: finalized anchor set = B-100 allowlist + B-102 proximity targets. Prerequisite to the cousin half of B-099 and to B-102.

items.label: cousin-color-detection
items.summary: Detect near-match cousin color literals that share a token's semantic purpose but not its exact value, via post-populator RGB-distance analysis grouped by purpose and property.
items.description: Catch literals that do NOT exact-match a token but are the same semantic color as one (cousins: reds `#f44747`/`#dc2626`/`#f14c4c`/`#ff4444`/`#f87171`, greens `#22c55e`/`#16a34a`/`#4caf50`/`#4bac52`... same job, non-identical values). Invisible to the exact-match matcher (clean, not drift) so they never surface for consolidation. Approach: post-populator SQL analysis, NOT a populator change -- all inputs are catalog columns at rest (`component_name` value, `variant_qualifier_1` property, file, selector). Parse each literal's RGB, distance to each anchor (B-101), label nearest-within-threshold as suggested target, NULL if nothing close. Group by (suggested_anchor, property) so the use-case boundary holds -- never merge text-green into background-green. RGB-Euclidean is crude but robust at the coarse level needed; fragile only where anchors sit close (teal-vs-green) -> eyeball. Depends on B-101. Distinct from B-100 (exact-match safety); B-102 is near-match candidates.

items.label: state-not-compounded
items.summary: Add STATE_NOT_COMPOUNDED to the CSS spec and build populator detection (state-token vocabulary plus non-compound styled-suffix check).
items.description: Add the drift code to xFACts_CSS_Spec section 18 and build populator detection (state-token vocabulary + non-compound styled-suffix check). Triggers a JBoss conversion pass when enabled.

items.label: endpoint-protection-default
items.summary: Codify the endpoint-protection default (GET via page access, mutating endpoints via Test-ActionEndpoint plus a registry row) and build an Asset_Registry gap report of non-compliant endpoints.
items.description: Asset_Registry catalogs 245 routes, ~54 mutating; only 19 have RBAC_ActionRegistry rows. Remaining 35 use manual `Get-UserAccess` (Pattern B) or no check (Pattern C). Codify the default: GET relies on page-level access; mutating endpoints must call `Test-ActionEndpoint` and have a registry row. Build a gap report from Asset_Registry to find non-compliant endpoints. (Per-page rows tracked under B-022.)

items.label: action-registry-rows
items.summary: Test-ActionEndpoint is wired but fail-open until registry rows exist; add RBAC_ActionRegistry rows for the mutating/launch endpoints that are UI-hidden but not server-enforced.
items.description: `Test-ActionEndpoint` is wired but fail-open until rows exist -- endpoints are admin-*hidden* via UI but not admin-*enforced* server-side. Add rows for: DM Operations launch/abort; JBoss `switch-server`; DBCC launch/abort; File Monitoring write endpoints (config save / webhook create / monitor save); Job Flow write endpoints (`app-tasks/toggle`, `app-tasks/batch`, `configsync/save`); Server Health kill-zombies. (BDL set tracked under B-003.) Also: DM Operations Archive launch not yet tested (Shell Purge confirmed live).

items.label: wildcard-gapcheck-fp
items.summary: Wildcard-granted admin pages show as false positives in the NavRegistry-vs-PermissionMapping gap-check; resolve query-side or add a requires-explicit-permission flag.
items.description: /admin and /platform-monitoring show as false positives in the NavRegistry-vs-PermissionMapping gap-check (they rely on Admin wildcard `*`). Decide between a query-side fix or a schema-side fix (add requires_explicit_permission BIT to NavRegistry).

items.label: data-retention-strategy
items.summary: No data retention exists for any module; design and implement a platform-wide per-table retention strategy run via an orchestrator-scheduled cleanup.
items.description: No retention exists for any module. Design and implement a strategy covering all historical tables: Activity DMV/XE snapshots, backup/disk/replication history, API_RequestLog, Teams/Jira RequestLog, Orchestrator TaskLog/CycleLog, index execution history, batch tracking history. Per-table retention by operational value; implement via an orchestrator-scheduled cleanup process.

items.label: pode-error-logging

items.description: No Pode error logs written for ~3 months despite error logging configured in Start-ControlCenter.ps1. Determine cause: log file permissions, directory cleanup job, rotation, or the prevalent empty `catch { }` blocks swallowing exceptions before Pode sees them. Restore visibility before production user expansion.

items.label: active-batch-eob-alert
items.summary: Add a daily end-of-business alert for manual PMT payment batches still ACTIVE (needs accounting input on cutoff time).
items.description: Daily alert for manual payment batches still ACTIVE at end of business day. Requires accounting team input on cutoff time.

items.label: shell-purge-validation-fatal
items.summary: Harden the Step 3d exception-validation path in Execute-DmShellPurge so failures are fatal (hoist $newExclusions, set the failed flag, log, Teams alert, break) instead of crashing or silently partial-filtering.
items.description: Step 3d try/catch in `Execute-DmShellPurge.ps1` documented "non-fatal" but actual failure ranges from script crash (if `$newExclusions` unset because temp table CREATE/populate failed) to silent partial exclusion filtering (check SQL failing mid-loop). Hoist `$newExclusions` outside the try. Make validation failures fatal: set `$Script:ValidationFailed`, write Failed batch log, fire Teams alert `TriggerType='SHELL_PURGE_VALIDATION_FAILED'`, break the loop. Replace empty inner catch on `#shell_exclusions` INSERT (~line 890) with a WARN. Identified 2026-05-01 pre-prod review; not blocking initial run (worst case is non-destructive crash).

items.label: shell-purge-noop-notice
items.summary: Three startup-path exits in Execute-DmShellPurge return silently; fire INFO/WARNING Teams notices so operators know the run was a no-op.
items.description: Three startup-path exits in `Execute-DmShellPurge.ps1` exit 0/1 with no Teams notice: (1) `Test-ShellPurgeAbort` true at top, (2) WFAPURGE workgroup not found in `crs5_oltp.dbo.wrkgrp` (Step 3a, exit 1), (3) Schedule returning 0/BLOCKED at startup (Step 1, exit 0). Each should fire INFO/WARNING so operators know the run was a no-op. Identified 2026-05-01 pre-prod review.

items.label: deptops-enrichment
items.summary: DeptOps still needs full documentation enrichment.
items.description: DeptOps needs full enrichment.

items.label: docs-rbac-nav
items.summary: Apply RBAC and dynamic nav to the /docs pages (currently unauthenticated) via auth on the static route and a doc_page_id -> route -> permission lookup.
items.description: Apply RBAC + dynamic nav to /docs/pages/*. Currently unauthenticated. Requires: (1) auth on the /docs static route in Start-ControlCenter.ps1, (2) doc_page_id -> CC page route -> permission lookup, (3) nav.js update for filtered registry. doc_page_id in RBAC_NavRegistry is the join key.

items.label: credential-model-docs
items.summary: Document the two-tier credential model and catalog script error-handling patterns, then fill the Development Guidelines credential and integration stubs currently marked PENDING.
items.description: Two-tier credential model is in production (master passphrase from GlobalConfig decrypts a per-service passphrase, which decrypts service values; `Get-ServiceCredentials` exists in both `xFACts-OrchestratorFunctions.ps1` for standalone scripts and `xFACts-CCShared.psm1` for CC routes). Error-handling patterns across scripts are not yet cataloged. Investigate both, then fill Development Guidelines section 3.7 and the section 7.1-7.3 integration stubs, which currently carry PENDING DOCUMENTATION placeholders.

items.label: shared-function-inventory
items.summary: Generate an always-current markdown inventory of shared PowerShell functions from Asset_Registry and repoint the Development Guidelines function lists at it.
items.description: Export the always-current list of SHARED PowerShell functions from `dbo.Asset_Registry` (file_type=PS, scope=SHARED, reference_type=DEFINITION, component_type=PS_FUNCTION) into a markdown reference (Platform_Registry.md sibling or a new file), grouped by file and sorted file then line_start; handle NULL `purpose_description` rows gracefully (see B-085). Pipeline already emits .md from queries, so small lift. Once built, repoint Development Guidelines section 3.6 (shared-resources function lists), the section 4.3 connection-helper table, and the section 3.6 forward-note at it. Distinct from B-008: that item generates CC docs-site *pages* (CC-scoped); this is a cross-zone markdown reference (includes standalone-zone shared functions) consumed by the Development Guidelines. Related to B-008 -- shared data source, different deliverable and audience.

items.label: user-context-dedup
items.summary: Home and likely other pages call both Get-UserAccess and Get-UserContext, redundantly re-resolving roles; investigate a module-level dedup (shared resolved roles or a superset return).
items.description: Home (and likely other pages) call both Get-UserAccess and Get-UserContext in the same request; each independently calls Confirm-RBACCache + Resolve-UserRoles and rebuilds overlapping context (DisplayName, IsDeptOnly, DepartmentScopes appear on both return shapes). Get-UserContext uniquely adds IsAdmin, UserDepartments, HasPlatformAccess; Get-UserAccess uniquely adds HasAccess/Tier + audit logging. Duplication is in-memory (cache-backed, no extra DB round-trip) so cost is low. Investigate a module-level dedup: share resolved roles between the two, or have one return a superset. Requires full call-graph reconciliation across all CC routes -- not a per-page change. Surfaced during Home spec conversion.

items.label: capability-inventory
items.summary: Evaluate adding a capability-inventory catalog category (network, WebSocket, storage, timers) for runtime APIs that carry no cross-reference or drift payload today.
items.description: Asset_Registry populators currently catalog calls selectively (cross-reference payload: class/ID/event refs; drift signals: eval/document.write/window-assignment; platform function def/usage). Runtime "capability" APIs that carry no cross-reference or drift payload are not captured today. Evaluate adding a capability-inventory category: network (`fetch`/`XMLHttpRequest`), WebSocket, client-side storage (`localStorage`/`sessionStorage`), possibly timers. Network calls the strongest candidate (data-flow/audit value). Criterion: does an inventory of where code exercises the capability carry actionable value (audit, debugging, data-flow understanding)? Surfaced 2026-06-21 from the docs-nav localStorage question -- collapse-state localStorage judged noise under current criteria, but the capability-inventory category was never considered when the inclusion list was set. Also need to remove any references and code specific to engine-events from all populators.

items.label: populator-coverage-docs
items.summary: Document each populator's capture coverage and inclusion criteria in the Development Guidelines so what earns an Asset_Registry row is knowable without reading populator source.
items.description: Audit each populator (JS, CSS, HTML, PS) inclusion list against its file type, then add an informational section (Development Guidelines, near the shared-resources/Asset_Registry references) explicitly stating what each populator does and does not catalog, and the criteria a construct must meet to earn a row (cross-reference resolution / drift signal / platform def-or-usage). Makes the implicit capture criteria knowable without reading populator source. Requires maintenance when populators change. Distinct from B-008 (which generates pages from catalog *data* -- what's *in* the catalog); this documents the populators' *selection logic* -- what they *choose* to catalog and why. Surfaced 2026-06-21.

items.label: history-tree-table
items.summary: Establish a shared year/month/day history-tree table construct so the per-page implementations converge, standardizing on the CSS-rotate expand chevron.
items.description: Establish a shared year/month/day history-tree table construct so the per-page implementations converge. Today each page defines its own prefixed month-summary table (bat-, bid-, jfm-, b2b-) following the same shape. Two divergent expand-chevron mechanisms exist: CSS-rotate (Batch, B2B) vs glyph-swap (BIDATA, Jobflow) -- standardize on CSS-rotate (single glyph in markup, state in a class, no scattered \u25B6/\u25BC literals) and migrate the swap pages. Sibling to B-051 (section-table chrome).

items.label: variance-baseline-config
items.summary: The 14-day variance baseline is hardcoded in BIDATAMonitoring-API.ps1 and echoed in the UI and doc; migrate it to GlobalConfig and update query, label, and doc together.
items.description: The step-duration variance baseline is hardcoded in `BIDATAMonitoring-API.ps1` as `DATEADD(DAY, -14, GETDATE())` and surfaced to users in the Current Build Execution "14 Day Var" column label and the cc doc page (bidata-cc.html). Migrate the window to a GlobalConfig value; when executed, update the API query, the UI column label, and the cc doc together so the number stays consistent across code, UI, and docs. Surfaced 2026-07-22 during the BIDATA metadata/doc audit.

items.label: session-metadata-review
items.summary: Adopt an end-of-session Object_Metadata review as standing Code-session practice, producing a drift-fix script for the objects touched that session.
items.description: At the close of each Code session, review `dbo.Object_Metadata` (and the module doc pages when the session's changes were substantial) for the objects touched during the session, and produce an update script for any drift found -- keeping documentation in step with code as it changes rather than in periodic sweeps. Candidate for promotion into CLAUDE.md as a standing rule once proven over a few sessions. Surfaced 2026-07-22 during the BIDATA metadata/doc audit.

items.label: docs-zone-realignment
items.summary: Docs zone spec-era realignment: shell architecture, family files, and conformity pass.
items.description: The docs zone pre-dates the specs and conformity passes; it needs a one-time alignment to the CC zone's reference architecture. Scope: true shell layering and naming (docs-base.css acts as shell; nav.js serves as implicit JS shell and carries the doc_buildHubCards page pocket), family-file realignment, extraction of the BDL Import Guide's embedded style block, and static-page HTML spec/populator coverage. The existing BDL-guide and static-page-investigation backlog items fold into this item's execution when it runs.

items.label: dead-code-detection
items.summary: Build cross-file dead-code/orphan detection for CSS classes, JS functions, and endpoints (resolver enhancement plus advisory drift code; after the migration).
items.description: Cross-file orphan/unreferenced detection for CSS classes, JS functions, endpoints. Resolver enhancement + advisory drift code + likely capture-time DDL. See DeadCode_Detection_Findings.md. After the migration completes.

items.label: html-sniff-coverage
items.summary: The HTML populator sniffs only three emission patterns, so concatenation-built markup is silently skipped; flag helper functions that contain HTML markers but produce zero catalog rows.
items.description: HTML populator discovers emitted HTML via three sniff patterns only; HTML built by `+` concatenation or otherwise not tripping the sniff is silently skipped. A clean catalog doesn't prove completeness. Proposed: flag helper functions whose bodies contain HTML markers (`<div`, `class=`, `id=`) but produced zero catalog rows.

items.label: inline-script-tag
items.summary: Decide whether all pages inline the literal cc-shared.js script tag (spec-correct, zero drift) and retire Get-PageScriptTagHtml.
items.description: Decide whether all pages inline the literal `<script src="/js/cc-shared.js">` (spec-correct, zero drift) and retire `Get-PageScriptTagHtml`. Confirm before the next page so the set stays consistent. Feeds helper-consolidation cleanup.

items.label: keyframe-prefix-normalize
items.summary: Normalize all FOUNDATION keyframe names to the cc- prefix and update every animation reference (naming pass, not a drift fix).
items.description: Shell mixes unprefixed legacy (`pulse`/`spin`/`fadeIn`/`page-refresh-spin`), `cc`-prefixed (`ccModalFadeIn`), and page-tagged (`adm-*`). Normalize all FOUNDATION keyframe names to `cc-` and update every `animation:` reference. Naming-convention pass, not a drift fix.

items.label: admin-pulse-collapse
items.summary: Collapse duplicate admin opacity-pulse keyframes into the shell's shared pulse, updating admin.css references.
items.description: `adm-pulse-red`/`-yellow` and `adm-pip-pulse-red`/`-yellow` are identical opacity pulses, and identical to the shell's `pulse`. Four of the nine could collapse to `pulse` (updating admin.css refs).

items.label: section-table-chrome
items.summary: Establish shared section-table chrome and map outlier page-local section tables onto it.
items.description: Establish shared section-table chrome, then map outlier page-local section tables (Job Flow's Live Activity / Execution History, Batch's `bat-*-table`, etc.) to it. Later phase.

items.label: conditional-markup-support
items.summary: The HTML populator only catalogs IDs inside a single here-string, so conditional admin markup loses ID resolution; design proper conditional-markup support so partially-gated pages can omit admin markup without drift.
items.description: HTML populator only catalogs IDs literal inside a single here-string, so conditionally-emitted admin markup (`if $ctx.IsAdmin`) loses ID resolution and a second here-string breaks DOCTYPE detection. A&I uses the interim hide-not-omit approach (admin markup always emitted, collapsed via `aai-hidden`; API enforces real security). Design proper conditional-markup support (populator + spec) so partially-gated pages can omit admin markup server-side without drift.

items.label: stale-tile-display
items.summary: Show a dimmed stale tile when a server's collection is stale instead of letting the tile vanish.
items.description: Show a dimmed "stale" tile when a server's collection is stale, instead of the tile silently vanishing.

items.label: base-role-count
items.summary: Decide whether four base platform roles is right given the site is roughly 90% view-only with most writes admin-gated.
items.description: PowerUser/StandardUser/ReadOnly differ marginally given ~90% of the site is view-only and most writes are admin-gated. Decide whether 4 base levels is right or some collapse. Not driven by an active issue.

items.label: per-tool-access-tables
items.summary: Decide whether per-tool access tables (Tools.AccessConfig / AccessFieldConfig) persist or a more expressive RBAC model subsumes them.
items.description: `Tools.AccessConfig`/`AccessFieldConfig` exist because RBAC couldn't express BDL's granularity. Decide if this pattern persists (per-tool access tables) or a more expressive RBAC model subsumes some of it. Field-level granularity may be too domain-specific for generic RBAC.

items.label: config-source-logging
items.summary: Enhance Initialize-Configuration across scripts to log whether each setting loaded from GlobalConfig or fell back to a script default, speeding config-failure diagnosis.
items.description: Enhance Initialize-Configuration in all scripts to log whether each setting loaded from GlobalConfig or fell back to script default. PMT collector is the reference. Speeds diagnosis when alerts fire due to config connection issues.

items.label: ms-description-removal
items.summary: Remove legacy MS_Description extended properties from all objects now that Object_Metadata is the sole documentation source.
items.description: Remove legacy MS_Description extended properties from all objects. Object_Metadata is the sole documentation source. Inert -- cosmetic, but removes confusion about which system is authoritative.

items.label: index-constraint-rename
items.summary: Four ServerOps tables were renamed to the Index_ prefix but their constraints and indexes still carry old names; rename to match (cosmetic).
items.description: Four ServerOps tables renamed to Index_ prefix but constraints/indexes still carry old names. Rename to match convention. Cosmetic only.

items.label: nonquery-dml-audit
items.summary: Audit older scripts using Invoke-XFActsQuery for non-SELECT DML and switch them to Invoke-XFActsNonQuery per the stated rule (fix opportunistically).
items.description: `Invoke-XFActsNonQuery` was added after many scripts were written. Older scripts use `Invoke-XFActsQuery` for INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE where no result set is needed -- works but is inefficient (allocates a SqlDataAdapter/DataSet/row iteration unnecessarily). Rule: use `Query` only for SELECT or INSERT/UPDATE with `OUTPUT INSERTED`; use `NonQuery` for all other DML/DDL. Fix opportunistically as scripts are opened. Grep: `Invoke-XFActsQuery -Query` followed by INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE with no OUTPUT clause. Fixed so far: DmOperations-API.ps1 abort endpoint (2026-04-13).

items.label: parallel-group-execution
items.summary: Enable parallel execution for same-dependency-group processes with no interdependencies (not currently a concern; sequential completes within cycle time).
items.description: Processes in a dependency group run sequentially. Enable parallel execution for same-group processes with no interdependencies. Not currently a concern -- sequential completes within cycle time.

items.label: diagnostic-button-home
items.summary: Find a home for the sp_DiagnoseServerHealth Diagnostic Report button removed from the Server Health page.
items.description: Find an appropriate home for the sp_DiagnoseServerHealth button (removed from the Server Health page).

items.label: metric-value-split
items.summary: Split .metric-value into separate gauge and card-number classes for independent font sizing.
items.description: Split `.metric-value` into separate classes for gauges vs. card display numbers to allow independent font sizing.

items.label: incident-history-page
items.summary: Build an Incident History page timelining correlated incidents from the Activity and XE tables with plain-English explanations.
items.description: Timeline of correlated incidents from Activity_IncidentLog and XE tables, plain-English explanations. Builds on sp_Activity_CorrelateIncidents. Needs a planning session for correlation rules.

items.label: incident-analyzer-enhance
items.summary: Enhance the Incident Analyzer with time-window clustering, cross-source correlation, and plain-English narrative generation.
items.description: Smarter correlation: time-window clustering, cross-source correlation, plain-English narrative generation. Major diagnostic enhancement.

items.label: xe-batch-pagination
items.summary: Collect-XEEvents materializes the full session backlog before insert; batch and paginate reads so catch-ups stream, commit progress, and stay resumable.
items.description: Collect-XEEvents reads an entire session backlog into memory before insert (single `Get-SqlData` materializes the full set). Fine for incremental runs; on large catch-ups it means a long silent phase, high memory pressure, and risk of memory/timeout failure. Batch/paginate (read N, insert, advance cursor, repeat) so catch-ups stream, commit progress, and are resumable. Collector-refactor phase.

items.label: exception-schedule-form
items.summary: Add a UI form/method to create Exception Schedule entries plus sp_AddExceptionSchedule with validation.
items.description: Input form/method for creating an Exception Schedule entry, plus the stored procedure to add entries with validation. Links the UI to the proc (or a direct insert).

items.description: Evaluate scheduling index maintenance runs through Orchestrator ProcessRegistry instead of manual launch.

items.description: Add source_server to Replication_PublicationRegistry; populate from sys.servers via publisher_id in the collector. Replace agent_name string parsing in CC card rendering with the stored value.

items.summary: Revisit min_failure_threshold so critical jobs bypass the threshold and alert on any failure.
items.description: Revisit min_failure_threshold for critical jobs. Proposed: critical jobs bypass threshold, alert on any failure.

items.description: Transient field, not useful when TaskLog has full error history. Evaluate whether anything reads it.

items.description: Evaluate sp_AnalyzeHistory and sp_PopulateConfig for a ConfigSync management panel: unconfigured-flow indicator, config modal with schedule/alert settings.

items.summary: Replace the Windows Task Scheduler dependency with xFACts-managed scheduling for flow execution.
items.description: Replace Windows Task Scheduler dependency with xFACts-managed scheduling for flow execution.

items.description: stts_cd 5 (CANCELLING) stuck-job detection/alerting after a period in status.

items.label: section-load-inline-error
items.summary: Consider reverting background section-load failures from the blocking cc_showAlert modal to a non-blocking inline state, reserving the modal for user-initiated actions.
items.description: Section loaders surface API failures via `cc_showAlert` (modal). Consider reverting to a non-blocking inline state for background loads, reserving `cc_showAlert` for user-initiated action failures (save/apply). Minor, behavior-only.

items.label: failed-count-line
items.summary: Restructure the Shell Purge history's parenthetical failed counts into a separate line (showing 0 when none); apply the same to the Archive side.
items.description: Shell Purge "History" shows failed counts as a parenthetical (`101 (1 failed)`) on day rows and the Today pane -- looks slapped on. Restructure to a separate line (showing `0` when none). Apply same to the Archive side.

items.label: dbnull-truthy-scan
items.summary: Scan for the DBNull-as-truthy pattern elsewhere now that ConvertFrom-DBNull exists (defensive; no known bugs).
items.description: `ConvertFrom-DBNull` was added to xFACts-Helpers (2026-05-15) and applied to Get-UserPageTier / Get-UserContext. The DBNull-as-truthy pattern likely exists elsewhere where DB values are evaluated in boolean contexts. Scan for `if (-not $row.X)`, `Where-Object { $_.X }`, `-not $_.X` against any `Invoke-XFActsQuery` value. No known bugs -- defensive cleanup.

items.label: history-tab-refresh
items.summary: Verify whether the BDL history panel auto-refreshes on tab-return, since cc_connectEngineEvents early-returns before registering the visibilitychange listener for no-engine pages.
items.description: `cc_connectEngineEvents` early-returns when `bdl_ENGINE_PROCESSES` is absent (BDL has no engine cards), before registering the visibilitychange listener that fires `bdl_onPageResumed`. Verify whether the history panel auto-refreshes on tab-return for a no-engine page. Page otherwise fully functional.

items.label: scoped-purpose-capture
items.summary: The PS populator captures .SYNOPSIS for platform-tier functions but not the single-line purpose comment SCOPED-tier and standalone functions use, leaving those rows' purpose_description NULL; capture it so the generated inventory is complete.
items.description: The PS populator captures the docblock `.SYNOPSIS` into `purpose_description` for PLATFORM-tier functions, but SCOPED-tier and standalone functions use a single-line `#` purpose comment instead of a docblock (CC_PS_Spec section 8.4), which the populator does not capture -- so those rows land with `purpose_description` NULL. Capture the mandated single-line comment into the same field so the generated function inventory (B-083) is complete across all shared files, not just platform-tier ones.

items.label: token-scale-design
items.summary: Refine the never-designed chrome token scales (spacing, color-tint, font-size) by deciding promote-vs-normalize per axis before the bulk literal tokenization, since the scale decisions change what the correct token is.
items.description: The shell token scales were never designed -- they are whatever the early/smaller page set happened to use when the legacy shared files were created, frozen as-is. Full-catalog frequency analysis (2026-06-09) shows the scales have diverged badly from actual usage: heavy values are used everywhere with NO matching token, so they sit as non-drifting page-local literals invisible to the drift system. Spacing examples: `10px` padding 194 uses/24 pages, `14px` padding 73/15, `2px` padding 66/23, `10px` gap 50/19, `5px` padding 37/11 -- all zero-drift today because the spacing scale (4/6/8/12/16/20) has no 5/10/14. `10px` as a generic spacing unit is the single most-used spacing value site-wide and is not a token at all. Same phenomenon on color-tint (rgba accent tints at 0.15/0.3 used as badge/pill surfaces, no purpose-correct token -- they match the banner/glow tokens only by value coincidence; see B-099) and font-size (`0.82em`/`0.85em`/`0.88em`/`0.92em`/`0.95em` and bare `18px` etc. with no token). CORE DECISION per axis: promote (add tokens to match real usage -- preserves every pixel, grows the scale, lights up drift to then burn down) vs normalize (snap off-scale values onto a deliberate clean scale -- changes appearance in hundreds of places, keeps the scale restrictive). The boundary/restrictiveness question and the promote-vs-normalize question are the same question: how few, how rhythmic should each scale be, and is appearance change acceptable to get there. Logical spacing bounds: bottom ~4px (1/2px are borders/hairlines, not spacing -- `--size-border-thin` already covers 1px); top ~20-24px (above that is layout-dimension, not spacing rhythm). CRITICAL CASCADE: every token promoted instantly fires drift on every matching literal site-wide (e.g. promoting `10px` lights up ~250+ rows in one populator run), which then must be tokenized to clear -- so promotions must be deliberate and sequenced, not batched. Instrument: the cross-page frequency query (value x property, total_site_wide, pages_used_on, drift_rows) is the promotion-candidate ranker; high total + zero drift = de-facto standard that should be a token. This is the FIRST-STEP item: refine the scales before doing the bulk B-099 literal tokenization and before resolving the remaining exact-match literal drift, since the scale decisions change what "the correct token" even is. Dedicate a fresh session; many decisions, expect it to expand. Related: B-099 (file-side burn-down) and B-014 (size sub-classification) are downstream of this. Today's residual literal drift (~137, of which the cleanly-resolvable exact-matches were left in place) is accepted as deliberate until this lands.

items.label: drain-aware-teardown
items.summary: The raw service-stop teardown force-clears running_count to zero while fire-and-forget processes are still running; make the teardown drain-aware or enforce draining via the Admin modal before stopping.
items.description: The `ShutdownRequested` teardown in `Start-xFACtsOrchestrator.ps1` (NSSM stop / Ctrl+C path) stops launching, abandons the current cycle, then force-clears `running_count = 0` for all processes and exits. This is not graceful: fire-and-forget processes already launched are not waited on, so the registry is reset to zero while those processes are still physically running -- they later call Complete-OrchestratorTask against a registry that already shows them stopped (decrement floors at 0). The genuinely-safe path is the modal-driven drain mode (stop launching, poll until `running_count` reaches 0, then stop the service); the raw service stop is effectively a foot-gun and should never be issued in normal ops. Options to evaluate: (1) make the ShutdownRequested teardown itself drain-aware -- wait for `running_count` to reach 0 (with a timeout) before clearing -- so even a raw stop is actually graceful; (2) document/enforce "always drain via the Admin modal before stopping the service." Surfaced 2026-06-15 during the Start-xFACtsOrchestrator shutdown-handler relocation.

items.label: sustained-polling-alert
items.summary: A single POLLING result is indistinguishable from a healthy in-progress build, so a chronic source-query outage is invisible; escalate to Teams only when source failures become sustained.
items.description: `Monitor-BIDATABuild.ps1` Step 4: when the source-server (DM-PROD-REP) msdb query fails, the cycle can't determine build state and reports `POLLING` (corrected 2026-06-17 from an invalid `WARN` that threw against `Complete-OrchestratorTask`'s ValidateSet). Correct for a transient blip -- the Agent job itself is unaffected, the monitor just couldn't observe it -- but a single `POLLING` is indistinguishable from a healthy in-progress build, so a *chronic* source outage is currently invisible. Desired: escalate when source-query failures become sustained (e.g. N consecutive failed cycles -> Teams WARNING) while staying silent on one-off blips. Needs a consecutive-failure count that survives across cycles; options weighed and deferred: (A) derive from history if a BIDATA table can record per-cycle "couldn't observe" outcomes without polluting build-history semantics -- verify schema first; (B) a small durable counter (GlobalConfig row or BIDATA state) incremented on failure, reset on first success, threshold-gated to `Send-TeamsAlert`; (C) "alert once per day on any failure" via dedup-keyed `TriggerValue` (simplest, no new state, but treats every blip as one ping). Threshold/window depend on the ProcessRegistry run interval (not yet confirmed). Low priority -- non-fatal and currently benign. Surfaced 2026-06-17 during the Monitor-BIDATABuild spec refactor.

items.label: partial-status-vocabulary
items.summary: Multi-target collectors have no honest single status when some targets fail (PARTIAL was invalid, now mapped to FAILED, losing nuance); evaluate a first-class PARTIAL/WARNING status across the orchestrator vocabulary and every engine branch that reads it.
items.description: A second collector (`Collect-JBossMetrics.ps1`, after `Monitor-BIDATABuild.ps1`'s `WARN`) reached for an orchestrator status the engine's vocabulary doesn't have: `Complete-OrchestratorTask` accepts only SUCCESS/FAILED/POLLING/NOT_STARTED, and a multi-target collector where some-but-not-all targets fail has no honest single value -- it was written as `PARTIAL` (invalid; threw against the ValidateSet) and is now mapped to FAILED (corrected 2026-06-17), with per-target detail preserved in the output summary. Collapsing partial to FAILED is correct and safe but loses nuance on the engine card (one app server of three failing reads identically to total collapse). Evaluate adding a first-class PARTIAL (and/or WARNING) status as a deliberate orchestrator-vocabulary extension: add to the `Complete-OrchestratorTask` ValidateSet *and* define its behavior in every engine branch that interprets `task_status` (card color/state, `last_execution_status`, `last_successful_date` scheduling logic, any status-bucketed dashboards). This is shared-contract work on `xFACts-OrchestratorFunctions.ps1` + the CC engine, not a per-script change. Related to the same vocabulary gap noted at B-091. Surfaced 2026-06-17 during the Collect-JBossMetrics spec refactor.

items.label: dimension-token-convention
items.summary: Replace the name-based chrome-dimension carve-out with a naming convention so only explicitly-prefixed general-dimension tokens participate in size literal-matching and structural/chrome dimensions match nothing.
items.description: Long-term successor to the B-097-implemented name-based carve-out. The CSS populator sub-classifies size literals into four purpose families so a literal matches only same-purpose tokens (size-spacing / size-radius / size-border / size-dimension; Get-TokenNameFamily, ~L956-959). The size-dimension family is a catch-all: every remaining --size-* token (the chrome/structural dimensions --size-nav-width, --size-nav-collapsed, --size-nav-head, --size-max-width) collapses into it, so a page-file width/height literal that value-matches one of those chrome tokens by coincidence fires a false DRIFT_PX_LITERAL -- exactly what the drift definition's own caveat ("a size value matching a token of unrelated purpose is not drift") says it should not. Surfaced 2026-06-22: docs-controlcenter.css .doc-mock-col-right width: 240px flagged purely because it equals --size-nav-width: 240px (the nav rail), an unrelated purpose. The immediate fix (implemented same session) carves the known chrome/structural dimension tokens by name into a family that matches nothing, mirroring the color side's property-specific-vs-role split (Get-TokenNameFamily ~L944-951) but with "matches nothing" rather than color-role's "matches anything." The name-based carve-out works today but needs the name list extended whenever a new chrome/panel/modal/slideup dimension token is introduced. This item is the durable replacement: define a convention so the matcher is name-list-free -- only tokens carrying an explicit general-dimension prefix (e.g. --size-dimension-* or --size-measure-*) participate in dimension literal-matching, and every other --size-* is purpose-specific and matches nothing. Requires a CC_CSS_Spec token-naming rule stating that general-reusable dimension tokens carry the prefix and structural/chrome dimensions do not; note there are zero general-dimension tokens today, so under the convention no width/height literal would match anything until a general token is deliberately introduced (arguably the correct state). Related to B-099 (docs radius scale folded into the site-wide burn-down) and the B-087 promote-vs-normalize scale work -- same underlying question of which values deserve tokens and at what purpose granularity.

items.label: bdl-guide-style-extract
items.summary: Extract the bdl-import-guide's embedded style block into a conformant docs CSS file and eliminate its inline styles (gated on whether a docs static-HTML populator is built).
items.description: The standalone BDL Import guide (pages/guides/bdl-import-guide.html) was brought to app-frame parity with the narrative pages (frame conversion, doc- prefix on all 107 page-local mock/guide/step classes and their compound state classes, structural classes mapped to the shell, breadcrumb dropped in favor of the rail, scripts wired) -- but its visual design still lives in a ~375-line embedded <style> block (3a: prefixed in place, not extracted) plus ~66 inline style="" attributes. Neither is spec-conformant: page CSS belongs in a catalogued .css file (not an embedded block), the block carries ~158 hex literals and element-descendant selectors (.guide-step h2, etc.) that the CSS spec forbids, and inline styles violate FORBIDDEN_INLINE_STYLE_ATTRIBUTE. None of this fires drift today because docs static HTML is not catalogued by any populator (same basis the cc pages' inline marker-positioning was left under Pair C "Option A"). 3b: extract the block into a real docs CSS file (e.g. docs-bdl-guide.css, or a guides-scoped stylesheet if more guides arrive -- see the guides/ folder support added to nav.js this session), refactored to full spec (hex->token, descendant-flatten to state-on-element, group-split, banners/file-org, byte discipline), and pull the ~66 inline styles into classes in that file. Also remove the dead state rules surfaced during 3a (doc-key-item, doc-active-cond, doc-selected-prod -- defined in the block but used by no element). Gate/sequence with the open question of whether a docs static-HTML populator is built; until then this is conformance-for-its-own-sake with no drift to burn down. Related: B-099 (docs radius scale folded into the site-wide burn-down), B-087 (scale promote-vs-normalize). Surfaced 2026-06-22 during the bdl-import-guide app-frame conversion (the last docs-zone page).

items.label: dbcc-no-work-status
items.summary: Execute-DBCC reports SUCCESS on no-work exits while its sibling Index scripts emit NO_WORK; add NO_WORK on both no-work paths for engine-card parity and correct the header .DESCRIPTION.
items.description: `Execute-DBCC.ps1` reports `SUCCESS` with a "No operations due" (or "No enabled DBCC schedules") output when nothing is due, rather than the `NO_WORK` orchestrator status its sibling Index scripts emit (`Execute-IndexMaintenance.ps1` sets `last_status = 'NO_WORK'` and returns `NO_WORK`). The script's comment-based-help header (.DESCRIPTION) still claims "exits NO_WORK." Add `NO_WORK` emission on both no-work exit paths for engine-card parity; the header then becomes accurate as-is. Docs (metadata design_note 3625, arch-page troubleshooting) were corrected to describe current SUCCESS behavior in the 2026-07-23 DBCC audit -- this item instead makes the code match the header's original intent. Also correct the header .DESCRIPTION line at that time. Surfaced 2026-07-23 during the DBCC metadata/doc audit.

items.label: queue-alert-api-decision
items.summary: Teams.sp_QueueAlert is an intentionally-exposed external T-SQL entry point with no current callers; decide whether to keep it as a documented external API or retire it.
items.description: No xFACts or known external callers (verified 2026-07-22: `sys.sql_expression_dependencies`, `sys.sql_modules` text search, and SQL Agent job steps all empty; the xFACts PowerShell caller base migrated to `Send-TeamsAlert` on 2026-04-28). The proc was deliberately designed as a T-SQL entry surface that external, non-xFACts processes could call (its Jira sibling `sp_QueueTicket` likely has live external callers today) -- so it is an intentionally exposed API with no current tenants, not an orphan. Decide: keep it as an available external API (document it as such) or retire it (drop the proc script, soft-delete its `Object_Metadata` rows, update `Object_Registry`, and remove `sp_QueueAlert` references from the Teams doc pages). Surfaced 2026-07-22 during the Teams metadata/doc audit.

items.label: sent-status-route
items.summary: Admin route filters Teams requests on status Sent, which no writer sets, so the route returns nothing; fix the route filter, then align the mirrored metadata query to match.
items.description: The Admin route that lists Teams requests filters on a `Sent` status that no writer in the Teams pipeline ever sets, so the route's status filter matches nothing and the list comes back empty. Fix the route filter to the status writers actually set, then align the mirrored metadata query -- the Object_Metadata copy of the route's query -- so the documented query and the live route stay in step. Surfaced during the Teams metadata/doc audit.

items.label: queue-date-columns
items.summary: ProcessedDate and LastRetryDate on the ticket queue are set identically in the same statement; evaluate retiring one.
items.description: On the Jira ticket queue, ProcessedDate and LastRetryDate are assigned the same value in the same statement, so the two columns always hold identical data. Evaluate whether both columns are needed or one can be retired. Design decision surfaced during the Jira metadata/doc audit; no functional defect.

items.label: static-page-populator
items.summary: Feasibility of extending HTML populator coverage to static doc-site pages.
items.description: Static doc-site pages are outside the HTML populator's scan scope (it reads HTML inside .ps1 files only) and have no spec authority. Investigate extending populator coverage to static pages; decide whether that means a static-page section in CC_HTML_Spec or a separate static-page spec. Input: the spec-applicability gap list produced during the backlog doc-site page build. Session findings 2026-07-23: the JS spec's single page-boot constraint (section 11.1 -- one doc_init per file, with the INITIALIZATION banner holding only that function) blocks a single shared one-off JS file from serving several pages unless a page-dispatch pattern is adopted; nav.js already does exactly this for the hub, guarding doc_buildHubCards behind a filename check. Zone one-off census taken the same session: docs-hub.css is the only single-page asset among the twelve docs CSS and JS files (every other file serves 14 or more of the 66 pages, and all twelve are registered SHARED in Object_Registry); zero pages carry an inline script block; and the BDL Import Guide is the only page carrying an embedded style block.
